{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''[…]'' I had a vision centuries ago that Kamino would unwittingly create a clone army for the dark side.|Kina Ha|<ref name="501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>}}
'''Vorusku''' was a [[Waterworld/Legends|waterworld]] located within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]]. It was the site of a [[Unidentified volcano city|volcano city]] as well as a floating [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] named the [[Hyperdrive Cantina]]. The [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kina Ha]] once lived on Vorusku. While there, she had a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the future in which the [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]] of the planet [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] would create an [[Army/Legends|army]] for the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].

Following the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], the Vorusku [[Crime lord/Legends|crimelord]] [[Jalla Spree]] supplied the [[Blue Star Pirates]] gang with advanced weapons. The [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|office]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] sent a group of individuals to discover the identity of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates']] supplier. Following a trail of leads to Vorusku, the group eventually uncovered Spree's role in supplying the pirates.

==Description==
Vorusku was a [[Waterworld/Legends|waterworld]]<ref name="Invasion" /> located within the [[Vorusku system/Legends|Vorusku system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Juris sector/Legends|Juris sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
[[File:Kina_Ha.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kina Ha once experienced a vision of the future on Vorusku.]]
[[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] before<ref name="Vorusku date">In ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'', [[Kina Ha]] states that, [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the events of the novel, she had a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] depicting the creation of a [[Kamino/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Army/Legends|army]] for the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' states Ha had that vision while living on Vorusku, which means she lived on that [[Planet/Legends|planet]] centuries before the events of ''Imperial Commando: 501st''.</ref> [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].</ref> Vorusku served as temporary residence for the unorthodox [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kina Ha]]. During the Kaminoan's stay there, she experienced a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the future in which<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> the [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]] of Ha's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] would unwittingly create an [[Army/Legends|army]] of<ref name="501st" /> millions of<ref name="GATORW" /> clone [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] for the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="501st" /> In the Jedi's vision, the soldiers loaded their weapons and were ultimately led by the dark side. The vision haunted Ha, causing her to return to Kamino.<ref name="GATORW" />

At some point after the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|ended]]<ref name="Invasion" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Vorusku [[Crime lord/Legends|crimelord]] [[Jalla Spree]] began to supply the [[Blue Star Pirates]] with military-grade weaponry.<ref name="Invasion" />

{{RPGseed}}
The [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Office of the Supreme Chancellor]] of the<ref name="Invasion" /> [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="Invasion" /><ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> recruited a group of individuals to discover the [[Color/Legends|Blue]] [[Star/Legends|Star]] [[Pirate/Legends|Pirates']] supplier. The group followed a lead to a hidden cove on a [[Unidentified jungle planet (Blue Star Pirates)|jungle planet]], where the individuals fought a pair of Blue Star Pirates. After the fight, the group discovered on one of the pirates a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] originating from the [[Hyperdrive Cantina]] on Vorusku.<ref name="Invasion" />

Traveling to the [[Water/Legends|water]] planet, the [[Agent/Legends|agents]] of the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor's]] office investigated the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], asking questions about the pirate group. That resulted in a brawl between the agents and a group of four thugs working for Spree. When the investigators eventually defeated Spree's underlings, the latter revealed that Spree possibly had information regarding the weapons used by the Blue Star Pirates.<ref name="Invasion" />

On their way to Spree's headquarters, the Republic agents were ambushed by a [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Vorusku)|bounty hunter]] in a [[Unidentified volcano city|volcano city]]. After a difficult confrontation, the group of individuals proceeded to finally discover Spree's role in arming the Blue Star Pirates. While the group attempted to escape with that information, the crimelord sent out a [[Droideka/Legends|destroyer droid]] to eliminate the individuals, which led to a fierce fight between the group and the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] in a cliffslide [[Slaver/Legends|slaver]] camp.<ref name="Invasion" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
Vorusku was the site of the floating bar named [[Hyperdrive/Legends|Hyperdrive]] Cantina as well as headquarters used by Jalla Spree. A [[Volcano/Legends|volcano]] [[City/Legends|city]] was located at some point along the route between the two. There was also a cliffside slaver camp on Vorusku.<ref name="Invasion" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|To create a bridge to the next encounter, you decide that one of the pirates has a datacard from the Hyperdrive Cantina on the water planet Vorusku—both names you've made up.|Vorusku is introduced in an example roleplaying game adventure|<ref name="Invasion" />}}
Vorusku was introduced in ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]'', a [[2000]] [[roleplaying game]] set authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] that served as an introduction to the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. Vorusku was mentioned in the ''Invasion of Theed Adventure Game'' rulebook as an example of a world made up by the gamemaster for use in a randomized adventure within the game. This article assumes the given adventure idea plays out as described.<ref name="Invasion" />

The existence of Vorusku within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was confirmed by its mention in the [[2004]] ''Star Wars Roleyplaying Game'' sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''.<ref name="GATORW" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Vorusku [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Vorusku,<ref name="TEA" /> in grid square P-17.<ref name="EAOC" />
